texto: The philosopher is like a man fasting in the midst of universal intoxication. He alone perceives the illusion of which all creatures are the willing playthings
autor: he is less duped than his neighbour by his own nature.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a he is less duped than his neighbour by his own nature.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The philosopher is like a man fasting in the midst of universal intoxication. He alone perceives …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, henri-frederic-amiel, philosopher pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
